architecture_fit.note: Hits canonical ceiling (0.5/0.5) but via a degenerate positional +/-1 lookup, not content-based matching, and not a base_model.py delta (raw numpy assignment, no QKV/softmax).

baseline_comparison.note: Relies entirely on the benchmark's built-in uniform baseline; no self-authored strawman or no-attention ablation showing where the method specifically wins.

faithfulness.note: No model and no causal/ablation evidence; README states it is hand-coded but proposes no faithfulness check.

operating_range.note: Sweeps the goal's prefix/suffix-len axis {1,2} and honestly shows degradation (robustness 0.5), but no ~2 orders of magnitude of scale and degradation is by-design.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Genuinely hand-set and reproduces canonical target, but via trivial direct array assignment rather than an explicit W_Q/W_K/W_V circuit.

visual_judgement.note: app.py is broken: uses np without importing numpy and has a mojibake variable name (head热), so it will not boot; heatmap also fabricates random positions.

visualisation_rationale.note: README justifies the heatmap axes mapping to the question, but the plotted data is randomly synthesized rather than the actual run's attention.

notes: A hand-built first pass that establishes a clean canonical ceiling (headline 0.5, lift 0.48 over the uniform baseline, zero distractor leak) and honestly reports degradation on multi-token slices. But the rubric-priority items are weak: the 'mechanism' is a degenerate positional +/-1 lookup rather than content-based wildcard matching and bears no resemblance to base_model.py, there is no self-authored baseline or causal/ablation evidence, and the app.py visualization is broken (missing numpy import, mojibake variable) so it will not boot.
